name: Light hadron spectrum from lattice QCD
requirement: Any candidate framework that includes QCD must reproduce the light-hadron spectrum (pion, kaon, proton, neutron, omega, ...) obtained from first-principles lattice QCD. The 2008 BMW result (Durr et al.) computed the masses of the lightest baryons from QCD with QED inputs only (m_u, m_d, m_s, alpha_s) and reproduced experimental values to ~1-2%.

regime.description: Strong-coupling QCD at the confinement scale; first-principles non-perturbative calculation.

what_it_excludes: Any framework that claims to replace QCD as the strong-interaction sector must reproduce these confinement-scale predictions. Lattice QCD has now matured to the point where the entire light-hadron spectrum, decay constants, and form factors are calculable with controlled uncertainties.

parameterization.name: Lattice QCD: discretize Euclidean QCD on a 4D space-time lattice with finite spacing a; take continuum limit a -> 0 and infinite-volume limit V -> infinity.
parameterization.summary: Inputs: bare quark masses m_u, m_d, m_s, gauge coupling g_s. Outputs: hadron masses in units of one chosen scale (e.g. f_pi or m_Omega).

procedural.predicted_observable: Mass of any hadron: M = function of bare-parameter inputs after taking continuum and chiral limits. For mesons: M_pi = 139.57 MeV, M_K = 493.677 MeV, M_eta = 547.86 MeV. For baryons: M_p = 938.272 MeV, M_n = 939.565 MeV, M_Lambda = 1115.683 MeV.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Lattice predictions match experiment at sub-percent for stable hadrons; few-percent for resonances.
framework_inputs: If the framework replaces or modifies QCD in any regime, predict the hadron spectrum or, equivalently, the strong-interaction running coupling and confinement mechanism.

primary_references.citation: Durr et al. (BMW), 'Ab Initio Determination of Light Hadron Masses', Science 322, 1224 (2008)
Borsanyi et al., 'Ab initio calculation of the neutron-proton mass difference', Science 347, 1452 (2015)
